signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD/CHF exhibits extreme G10 divergence as NZD leads weekly performance (+1.438% avg across 4 pairs) while CHF lags (-0.707% avg across 6 pairs), generating a 2.145% G10 spread. RBNZ 2.5%. Despite a negative real rate differential (NZD -0.58% vs CHF -0.62%), the nominal carry advantage and RBNZ hiking stance support NZD/CHF buyers near one-month highs. The next RBNZ policy decision on September 2 is the primary catalyst to sustain or unwind this divergence.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Carry Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 162.37, holding above the critical 160.00 level where BoJ verbal and physical intervention risks historically escalate. Fed 3.75%. This geopolitical tension clashes with a massive +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%). JPY speculative positioning remains heavily short with COT LF net short at -104,231, exposing the pair to rapid short-squeeze risk if geopolitical escalation triggers safe-haven flows. However, with VIX at 16.7 (normal volatility), carry dominance persists until VIX crosses 25. The next Fed policy decision on July 29 determines if the yield gap remains wide.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Volatility Premium
signals.text: GBP/USD trades at 1.3480 with COT LF net positioning showing specs are net long +17,228 (59.0% long). However, the options market is pricing in significant hedging demand, with GBP/USD 1M ATM IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.0%, generating an elevated Vol Risk Premium (VRP) of +5.0%. This high premium, combined with a mild put skew (25d RR at -0.49), indicates that institutional market participants are actively hedging downside risk rather than chasing the weekly spot gain (+0.48%). This options-spot divergence suggests late-trend exhaustion for Sterling. The next BoE policy decision on July 30 serves as the key directional catalyst.

signals.title: GBP/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme vs COT
signals.text: GBP/CHF retail sentiment has reached an extreme 91% short / 9% long, presenting a strong contrarian buy signal. This retail positioning directly diverges from institutional flow, where GBP COT LF net positioning is long (+17,228) and CHF is net short (-8,638). This institutional-retail divergence is fundamentally supported by a massive +375bp carry premium favoring GBP (BoE 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). Retail traders attempting to fade the GBP/CHF uptrend face severe capitulation risk if spot extends higher. The next BoE policy decision on July 30 is the key event to watch for confirmation.
